t_ch: 「死亡」的存在会被家族掩盖，他打算从什么地方破局？切入点必须足够重要，所有人都会关注，但过程中又不能太张扬……
t_en: The existence of "Death" will be covered up by The Family, so how does he plan on taking them down? It's got to be something important enough that everyone will notice, but it also can't be anything too out-in-the-open...

t_ch: 会是「酒店」吗？
t_en: Perhaps he's going for the Hotel?

t_ch: 对住客下手吗？不像。匹诺康尼的客人有不少寰宇皆知的大人物，即便是公司也不敢轻易动这些人。砂金是个精明的商人，不可能不明白这点。
t_en: An attack on the hotel guests? Unlikely. Penacony's guests include quite a few big wigs known throughout the whole cosmos, people who not even the IPC would dare take lightly. Aventurine is a shrewd merchant, and there's no way he doesn't know that.

t_ch: 会是「家族」吗？
t_en: Perhaps he's going for The Family?

t_ch: 他的目标一定是家族，但方法不得而知。「同谐」在匹诺康尼势力壮大，想正面与之抗衡非常困难。公司指派砂金前来，也说明了他们不打算依靠正常手段。
t_en: He's definitely going for The Family, and it's just a matter of how. The Harmony is strong in Penacony and almost impossible to take on head-to-head. The fact that the IPC dispatched Aventurine shows that they do not intend to simply play by the book here.

t_ch: 会是「黄泉」吗？
t_en: Perhaps he's going for Acheron?

t_ch: 砂金对她投入了相当多的关注。但对这位巡海游侠…我们目前知之甚少，做不出什么判断。
t_en: Aventurine has devoted considerable attention to her. But this Galaxy Ranger... we know hardly anything about her and can't rush to any conclusions.

t_ch: 会是「星穹列车」吗？
t_en: Perhaps he's going for the Astral Express?

t_ch: …我也在考虑这个可能性，尤其是…他对你格外看重，屡次刻意接近。或许他也不清楚你的虚实，还在反复试探。
t_en: ...I was also considering this possibility, especially... because he respects you so much, and has sought you out before a few times. Perhaps he's also unsure of your intentions, and is probing you.

t_ch: 现在也得不出什么结论。
t_en: No conclusions can be drawn just yet.

t_ch: …现在也就是随便想想。无论如何，和砂金打交道都得保持警惕，他很擅长把握人心，懂得什么时候该出手。并且……
t_en: ...I'm just speculating. In any case, we have to be careful when handling Aventurine. He's skilled at reading people and discerning the right moment to strike. Also...

t_ch: 他显然是个天生的赌徒，愿意为胜利押上所有。
t_en: He's clearly a born gambler if he's willing to go all in to win.

t_ch: 砂金想要扳倒家族，就必须制造出足够大的破绽。他至今为止的行动…目的究竟是什么……
t_en: If Aventurine wants to bring down The Family, he'll have to create a big enough chance. After everything he's done so far — what is his goal...?

t_ch: 看来砂金没有更多要求，我们就先处理家族的委托吧。姬子，你认为呢？
t_en: Looks like Aventurine doesn't need anything else — let's turn our attention to The Family's assignment for now. Himeko, what do you think?

t_ch: 目前的线索里，{NICKNAME}目击到的两起命案最为直接，我建议从这里入手。
t_en: Among our current clues, the two murders that {NICKNAME} witnessed are the most directly connected. I suggest starting here.

t_ch: 有件事我很好奇，如果一个人在梦中死亡，那现实中的他呢？既然有家族授意，我们不如先返回现实，向酒店核实流萤小姐的情况…也可以顺便打探下知更鸟小姐的消息。
t_en: One thing I'm curious about is, if a person dies in a dream, what happens to them in real life? Seeing as we're at The Family's behest, why not pop back out to reality and verify Miss Firefly's situation back at the hotel... Perhaps we could also make a few inquiries about Miss Robin while we're out there.

t_ch: 那这样如何？我们兵分两路，梦境中也有些令人在意的事，我想先去调查，稍后再与你们会合。
t_en: How about we split off into two groups? There are still some things worth focusing on inside the Dreamscape — I'll investigate those and we can link up again later.

t_ch: 令人在意的事？…哦，没问题，那就拜托你了。
t_en: Worth focusing on? ...Oh, no problem. I'll leave it to you then.

t_ch: 欸？啊…还以为终于能看见姬子和杨叔同行了呢，那杨叔多注意安全！
t_en: Huh? Ah... I thought I'd finally get to see Himeko and Mr. Yang go out on a mission together. Oh well, take care then, Mr. Yang!

t_ch: 嗯。保持联络。
t_en: I will. Keep in touch.

t_ch: 尊贵的客人，可否出来一见？
t_en: Honored guest, could you come out for a second?

t_ch: 被这么盯着，我也是会感到为难的。
t_en: I'd be embarrassed too, getting stared at like that.

t_ch: 失礼了。我叫瓦尔特•杨，星穹列车的一员，相信你已见过我的同伴了。
t_en: Forgive me. My name is Welt Yang — I'm one of the Crew Members on the Astral Express. I believe you've met my colleagues.

t_ch: 「瓦尔特」……
t_en: Welt...

t_ch: 这个名字怎么了？
t_en: ...Is there something about my name?

t_ch: …在那之前，你不问问我的名字么？
t_en: ...First, don't you want to know my name?

t_ch: 恐怕不用了，黄泉小姐，你现在是匹诺康尼的知名人物。
t_en: I already do, Miss Acheron. You're a prominent figure in Penacony.

t_ch: …他们是怎么说的？
t_en: ...What are they saying about me?

t_ch: 有人声称你是这起连环命案的真凶——前来赴宴的泯灭帮同样惨死在你的刀下，而今，你试图在匹诺康尼掀起又一场腥风血雨。
t_en: Some claim that you're the real culprit behind these murders... that the Annihilation Gang's tragic fate at the banquet was a result of your blade... and that you're now attempting to unleash another bloodbath on Penacony.

t_ch: 泯灭帮？
t_en: The Annihilation Gang?

t_ch: 「永火官邸」的阿弗利特。
t_en: Ifrit of Ever-Flame Mansion.

t_ch: 「惨死」…那位大公以将死之躯化为烈火，舍身殉道。他是坚定、壮烈的命途行者，即便是恶徒，也不应受到如此诋毁。
t_en: "Tragic fate"... That duke turned his dying body to flames and sacrificed his life as a martyr. He was a determined and heroic Pathstrider. Not even a villain should be disparaged like this.

t_ch: 更何况，应邀而至的可疑人物不在少数。他们当真觉得…一柄长刀要比你手中的「黑洞」更危险么？
t_en: And what's more, there were plenty of suspects invited. Do they really think... that a blade is more dangerous than that "black hole" you're wielding?

t_ch: …很敏锐的直觉，就连家族也没能点出这把手杖的真面目。
t_en: ...Keen intuition. Not even The Family managed to point out the truth behind this cane.

t_ch: 所以你一定也清楚，黄泉小姐，窥视黑洞不是明智之举，作为一名潜在的危险分子，你对我们的了解已经到了令人不适的地步——
t_en: So you must surely know, Miss Acheron, that peering into a black hole is not a wise move. As a potential threat, your knowledge of us has reached uncomfortable depths...

t_ch: 亮明真身，表明来意。否则，我得请你做好被引力撕裂的准备了。
t_en: Reveal your true identity and intentions... Otherwise, brace yourself for gravitational disintegration.

t_ch: 那种事应该不会发生…但如果能让各位无名客少些防备，我乐意效劳。
t_en: That shouldn't be necessary... but if it makes the Nameless feel less defensive, I'll be happy to abide.

t_ch: 无论你是否相信，巡海游侠，黄泉…这就是我如今的身份。而拜访匹诺康尼，只是为了一个久远的「遗愿」。
t_en: Believe it or not, Galaxy Ranger, Acheron... those are the names I go by to this very day. My trip to Penacony is solely to fulfill an old "final request."

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>我为「钟表匠的遗产」而来…</color>就只是这样。我想自己已经足够坦诚。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>I'm here for the Watchmaker's Legacy...</color> and that's it. I think I've been honest enough.

t_ch: 你还是不愿说出自己的真实身份？
t_en: Still unwilling to reveal your true identity?

t_ch: 不是不愿，而是不能…我走过的路太长，对于加诸此身的种种，三言两语无法言明。
t_en: It's not that I don't want to. It's just that I can't... I've come so far, and I can't sum up all of that in just a few words.

t_ch: 每个人都有难以启齿的过往，不愿轻易示人的秘密…我也不会多问，星穹列车为何要带着一颗*星核*漫游银河。
t_en: Everyone has their own unspeakable past, secrets that they don't want to be revealed... and I won't be asking any more questions, such as why the Astral Express is roaming around the cosmos with a Stellaron on board.

t_ch: {M#他}{F#她}还好么，那位忆者…没有做什么吧？
t_en: Is {M#he}{F#she} okay? That Memokeeper... didn't do anything, right?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}没有危险。回到我们刚才的话题吧，能否得到我的信任，取决于你愿意袒露多少。
t_en: {NICKNAME}'s fine. Let's stick with the topic. Gaining my trust all depends on how much you're willing to reveal.

t_ch: 为了寻找那份「遗产」，入住匹诺康尼后我便走访各个梦境，进行了许多调查，期间也和不少来客产生过接触。这一过程中，我逐渐意识到……
t_en: I've run around many different Penacony dreamscapes just to try and find that "Legacy," and during this period I came into contact with quite a few guests. In the process, I gradually came to realize...

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的秘密…也许与曾经的「开拓」息息相关。
t_en: The secret of Penacony... may be closely related to the Trailblaze.

t_ch: 因此，我前来寻求各位的帮助。我没有足够的证据，但想提出一种可能：<color=#dbc291ff>一切悲剧的源头正潜伏于家族中</color>。如果你愿意信任我…我们可以一起找到用以佐证的事实。
t_en: That's why I've come to ask for your help. I don't have enough proof yet, but I'd like to speculate something: <color=#dbc291ff>the source of all tragedy lies within The Family</color>. If you could trust me... we could find the proof to support this claim, together.

t_ch: 瓦尔特先生，我认为你早就得出相同的结论了。
t_en: Mr. Yang, I think you've come to the same conclusion, haven't you?

t_ch: …就到这里吧。我暂且相信你没有敌意。
t_en: ...Let's leave it at that. For now, I'll choose to believe that you bear no hostility.

t_ch: 和我分享你的发现吧——就你我二人，在找到确切的证据前，我不想用模棱两可的揣测干扰其他人的判断。
t_en: Share your findings with me, and me alone. I don't want vague conjecture to interfere with other people's judgments before we find solid proof.

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Mhmm.

t_ch: …对了，要喝点什么吗？出发前，来两杯*如梦初醒*如何…不，四杯吧。
t_en: ...By the way, would you like something to drink? Before we go, how about two cups of Wake the Heck Up...? No, four cups.

t_ch: 因为接下来的对话…会持续很久。
t_en: Because the conversation coming up... will last forever.

t_ch: 我注视她很久了，第一次「邀约」是在酒店的宴会厅。
t_en: I've been watching her closely for a while now, and the first "invitation" was in the banquet hall of the hotel.

t_ch: 她坐在人群的角落，默不作声，只是喝着*如梦初醒*，一杯、两杯…我说这种饮料辛辣、苦涩，不是美梦的滋味，只适合那些厌倦了苏乐达的人。可她却说……
t_en: She just sat in one corner keeping silent, chugging down a couple cups of Wake the Heck Up... I told her it's a pungent, bitter beverage, not the taste of sweet dreams — only for people allergic to SoulGlad. And she said...

t_ch: 「是么？但在我尝来…它们并无区别。」
t_en: "Really? But I don't taste... any difference at all between them."

t_ch: 现实中的客房倒是意外朴素……
t_en: The guest rooms are charmingly minimalist...

t_ch: …就像你的外在一样，黄泉小姐。
t_en: ...An aesthetic you share, Miss Acheron.

t_ch: 得来全不费工夫。就是这只八音盒——泯灭帮收到的「邀请函」……
t_en: It's a cinch. This music box — the "invitation" received by the Annihilation Gang... There are latent memories that linger on it yet.

t_ch: 有关你的记忆不只属于你——我所知甚多，亦预言更远——只要用点手段，死者也能开口说话。
t_en: You see, memories of you are not yours alone. They travel in other people... Other things. I know much, and I can predict even more. With some help, the dead can be made to speak.

t_ch: 泯灭帮，那群遇见你后便不知去向的亡命徒…他们究竟经历了什么？让我来揭示吧。
t_en: The Annihilation Gang, that band of desperadoes who all disappeared after meeting you... What exactly happened to them? Well, let me reveal all.

t_ch: 「…十二刻度██十二梦境██父亲██我把它献予你…」
t_en: "...Gradation twelve ██ Dreamscape twelve ██ Father ██ I dedicate this to you..."

t_ch: 「…做得好██杜布拉██无论他们逃往何处██泯灭终将造访…」
t_en: "...Well done ██ Dubra ██ wherever they go, ██ shall be met by annihilation..."

t_ch: …有了，尽管很朦胧，但这是阿弗利特的声音。另一位…是他的子嗣吧。
t_en: ...There it is — it's hazy, but it's Ifrit's voice. The other one... is probably his progeny.

t_ch: 这是「邀请函」最初被交付时残留的记忆…片刻就中断了。那么之后是……
t_en: This is the residual memory from when the 'invitation' was first delivered... they were abruptly interrupted. Then, what happened next is...

t_ch: 「…他们躲入睡乡██但求安眠不被风雨搅扰…」
t_en: "...They sought refuge in the land of sleep ██, merely wishing for undisturbed rest, away from the storms..."

t_ch: 「…火焰的子嗣们██这是你们的成人之礼…」
t_en: "Children of the flame ██ this marks your rite of passage."

t_ch: 「…轮不到她出场████我一人便足够…」
t_en: "She won't be necessary ████ I alone am enough."

t_ch: 「…嘘████行于██毁灭██的亡命徒██什么时候怕过死亡…」
t_en: Shh—████ when have those ██ on the Path of Destruction ██ feared death?

t_ch: 「永火官邸」启程了，可怜的人们…他们还不知道等待在前方的是什么。
t_en: The Ever-Flame Mansion has set out on a journey. Those poor people... They have no idea what lies in wait ahead of them.

t_ch: 但记忆的复现很顺利，她要来了…很快就要来了…这里没有别人，不那么优雅也可以…我得不遗余力……
t_en: Memory recovery is going well, but slowly. She'll be here soon, and time is short.... There's nobody else here, so there's no need to be delicate... In fact I think I'd better go all out....

t_ch: …怎么回事？
t_en: ...What happened?

t_ch: 之后的记忆…是一片空白？怎么可能…这只八音盒落入黄泉之手，被她带来匹诺康尼是事实，本该这样的…可中间的过程……
t_en: The memory after that... is blank? How is that possible... This music box fell into Acheron's hands, and she brought it to Penacony — that's a fact, and that's how it should have gone... but along the way...

t_ch: …就像是被谁抹去了？谁做的……
t_en: ...It's like it's been erased? Who's done this...
"...Who are you?"

t_ch: …这是？
不对——这不是一段「记忆」？
t_en: No — is this not a "memory"?

t_ch: 「哦…一位忆者，你侍从流光忆庭…还是焚化工？」
t_en: "Oh... a Memokeeper. Do you serve the Garden of Recollection... or The Cremators?"

t_ch: 「我的名字是康士坦丝…很高兴认识你。我们本该在匹诺康尼相遇，共度一段…*刻骨铭心*的时光。」
t_en: "My name is Constance... A pleasure to meet you. We were supposed to meet in Penacony and spend an... unforgettable time together."

t_ch: 「但那似乎无法实现了。宴会之星并不欢迎大丽花，我也不需要成人礼…而你，我知道你在寻找什么……」
t_en: "But that seems unrealistic. Dahlia's not welcome on the Banquet Star, and I don't need a coming-of-age ceremony... And you, I know what you're looking for..."

t_ch: 「想要『她』的秘密？我可以给你，然后…替我享受那场盛会吧。」
t_en: "Want 'her' secret? I can give it to you, and then... you can enjoy the banquet for me."

t_ch: 「祝你留下『难忘』的回忆。」
t_en: "I wish you 'unforgettable' memories."

t_ch: …电话？要听听看吗？
t_en: ...A phone? Want to listen in?

t_ch: 「…日前，星际和平公司在松波尔的市场开拓任务已接近尾声——」
t_en: "...Days ago, the Interastral Peace Corporation's market trailblaze mission in Sombor was coming to an end..."

t_ch: 「松波尔全民庭的代表舍特康努格宣布，松波尔全体星区在庭会议上一致通过决议，将以观察员实体身份加入星际和平会议——」
t_en: "The representative of the Citizen Court of Sombor, Schattenkornug, announced that the entire Star District of Sombor had unanimously adopted a resolution at a meeting of the Court to join the Interstellar Congress as an observer entity..."

t_ch: 「市场开拓部主管奥斯瓦尔多•施耐德称，此举将『为争取松波尔全体公民的基本自由打下坚实基础』，并对公司在无主星区茨冈尼亚的开疆工作作出重要示范……」
t_en: "The head of the Marketing Development Department, Oswaldo Schneider, claimed that this move would 'lay a solid foundation to fight for the fundamental freedoms of all citizens of Sombor' and serve as an important demonstration of the company's pioneering work in the unclaimed region of Sigonia..."

t_ch: 卡卡瓦夏，你去哪儿了…你受伤了？！
t_en: Kakavasha, where did you go... Are you injured!?

t_ch: 我把它拿回来了，姐姐。
t_en: I got it back, Sister.

t_ch: 你去找他们了…？太危险了！这只是一串项链，不能吃，也不是水，没有它我们也能活下去。
t_en: You went to look for them...? That's too dangerous! It's just a necklace. It's neither food nor water. We can survive without it.

t_ch: 但我不能没有你，弟弟…不要再靠近那群{RUBY_B#剥皮刀}卡提卡{RUBY_E#}人了，好吗？
t_en: But I can't live without you, little brother... Promise me not to look for those {RUBY_B#Shellslasher}Katicans{RUBY_E#} again, okay?

t_ch: 姐姐，不要怕。卡提卡人很笨，但我很聪明，和他们玩「游戏」，赢的一定是我。
t_en: Sister, don't be afraid. The Katicans are fools, but I'm smart. I played a "game" with them, and I won.

t_ch: 「赢」？到底发生什么事了？告诉我。
t_en: Won? What happened exactly? Tell me.

t_ch: 我和他们打了赌，赌沙漠里的两只小鸟，还有我，哪个会先死掉。我赢了。
t_en: I made a bet with them — the two birds in the desert and me, who will die first? I won.

t_ch: 他们怀疑我出千，但我没有…我赢得光明正大。
t_en: They suspected me of cheating, but I didn't... I won fair and square.

t_ch: 我知道你能赢…你一直是个运气很好的孩子，你的幸运是{RUBY_B#芬戈-比约斯}地母神{RUBY_E#}的恩赐……
t_en: Of course. Of course you'd win... You've always been a lucky child. {RUBY_B#Fenge Biyos}Gaiathra Triclops{RUBY_E#} must be watching over you...

t_ch: 可这不是你去找卡提卡人硬碰硬的理由，他们嗜血、残忍，贪得无厌——别忘了爸爸妈妈……
t_en: But that's no reason to push your luck by going up against those... those bloodthirsty, CRUEL Katicans! Have you forgotten how Mom and Dad...!

t_ch: 这只是一串项链…可是卡卡瓦夏，你是我最后的家人了。
t_en: Look, this is just a necklace. But Kakavasha, you are my only family.

t_ch: 姐姐，对不起…我以为你会开心的。因为这是妈妈留给你的项链……
t_en: I'm sorry, Sister... I thought you'd be happy. Because mom left you this necklace...

t_ch: …以后不会再有了。
t_en: ...There'll be no next time.

t_ch: 它是很重要，但更重要的是你，我最爱的弟弟…我不会责怪你，但你要记得妈妈说过的话……
t_en: It is important, but not as important as you, my dearest brother... I don't blame you, but you must remember what mom said...

t_ch: 痛苦和贫穷是母神的考验，祂也赐给了我们机遇，那就是你的幸运，卡卡瓦夏。你的好运是我们…也是所有埃维金人最宝贵的财富。
t_en: Pain and poverty are the trials of Gaiathra Triclops. She has also granted us a chance, and that's your good luck, Kakavasha. Your good luck is the most precious wealth we — all Avgin have.

t_ch: 你是{RUBY_B#卡卡瓦夏}受母神赐福的孩子{RUBY_E#}，你能带领氏族走向幸福。所以，永远记得保护好自己，也永远不要怨恨痛苦和贫穷…好吗？
t_en: You're a child blessed by {RUBY_B#Kakavasha}Gaiathra Triclops{RUBY_E#} and can lead the clan to happiness. So, always remember to protect yourself and never resent the pain and poverty you're going through... All right?

t_ch: 听话，向母神发誓。
t_en: Listen to me, and swear to Gaiathra Triclops.

t_ch: …好。我向母神发誓，我会永远保护好这份「财富」……
t_en: ...Okay. I will swear to Gaiathra Triclops to protect this "wealth"...

t_ch: 可是，姐姐…如果三重眼的地母神真的在注视我们……
t_en: But Sister... If Gaiathra Triclops was really watching over us...

t_ch: 那当爸爸被流沙卷走的时候，母神为什么没有保佑他…明明爸爸是为了准备给她的供品，才会去卡提卡人在的地方……
t_en: Then why did she not protect Dad when he was swept away by the quicksand...? After all, Dad went to the Katicans' land only to prepare for Gaiathra Triclops' offerings...

t_ch: 当妈妈在我们怀里慢慢变冷的时候，母神又在哪里…妈妈直到闭眼的那一刻，口中还在请求她的原谅……
t_en: And where was Gaiathra Triclops when Mom was shivering in our arms? Mom was still pleading for Gaiathra Triclops' forgiveness under her breath until the moment she closed her eyes...

t_ch: 姐姐，大家都说我聪明，可我不明白…如果每一场雨都是母神的宽恕和恩赐……
t_en: Sister, everyone praises me for being smart, but I don't get it... If every rainpour was Gaiathra Triclops' forgiveness and grace...

t_ch: 那我们是犯了多少错误…才要为了死亡而出生在这世上…？
t_en: Then how bad were our sins? ...So much so that we were born in this world of death...?

t_ch: 我们到了。朝露公馆，橡木家系的要塞，各位家主共议匹诺康尼大计的地点。
t_en: We're here. The Dewlight Pavilion is the Oak Family's fortress and a place where heads of the families meet to discuss great plans for Penacony.

t_ch: 要塞？这比喻不错。前不久我才和伊伊玛尼喀星系的军阀打过交道，他们的同步轨道庄园都没这么戒备森严。
t_en: Fortress? I like this metaphor. I had dealt with the warlords of the Iymanika star system not long ago, and their synchronized orbital manor wasn't this heavily guarded.

t_ch: 和它的主人很相称，这宅子名义上属于星期日，没有他的邀请，普通宾客一辈子都没机会踏足这里。多看两眼吧，趁你还有片刻自由。
t_en: This mansion nominally belongs to Sunday, and is very befitting of its owner. Without his express invitation, the likes of ordinary guests would never grace these grounds in their lifetimes. Look around while you still have this moment of freedom.

t_ch: 嘿，教授——这话说的，你到底站在哪边？
t_en: Hey, Doc — Whose side are you on anyway?

t_ch: 谁能保证我不会出卖你呢？
t_en: Who's to say I won't sell you out?

t_ch: 那就拭目以待吧。等见到那位控制欲溢出的{RUBY_B#星期日}橡木家主{RUBY_E#}，我自有办法从他嘴里撬出答案。
t_en: We'll see. When we meet the {RUBY_B#Sunday}authoritarian master{RUBY_E#} of the Oak Family, I'll pry an answer out of him.

t_ch: 跟我走，我带你去他的会客厅。别说多余的话，家族的人交给我来应付。
t_en: Follow me, and I'll bring you to his parlor. Hold your tongue and let me deal with the members of The Family.

t_ch: 嘿，你们两个！前方是议事要地，非请勿入。
t_en: Hey, you two! That's a place of business. No entry!

t_ch: 我应星期日先生要求将嫌疑人带来了。「拉帝奥」，他应该吩咐过这个名字。
t_en: I was requested by Mr. Sunday to bring him the suspect. My name is Ratio. He should've mentioned it to you.

t_ch: 哦，我记得你…维里塔斯•拉帝奥，你的「庞奇虚粒子钟」令人印象深刻。
t_en: Oh, I remember you... Veritas Ratio. Your "Punch Virtual Particle Clock" is impressive.

t_ch: …你在说什么？
t_en: ...Excuse me?

t_ch: 就你脑袋上这个！虽然比不过我——「机动骑士」的全领域歼击动力铠。
t_en: The one on your head! Of course, it's nothing compared to my... full pocket-dimensional annihilating power armor of the "Mobile Knights."

t_ch: 上次我就提醒过你，你那身幻想战衣根本就不存在。
t_en: Right, and as I mentioned, that fantasy raiment of yours doesn't exist.

t_ch: 只是你看不见罢了！我说过，只有{RUBY_B#「家族」成员}「家人」{RUBY_E#}才能目视「机动骑士」的光辉——好了，快过去吧，别让星期日先生等急了。
t_en: That's because you can't see it! Like I say, only {RUBY_B#Member of The Family}Family{RUBY_E#} can see the glory of the "Mobile Knights." Enough, get going. Don't keep Mr. Sunday waiting.

t_ch: 唉…这里的白痴指数比外头也好不了多少。
t_en: *sigh*... It seems like the idiocy index here is no better than it is out there.

t_ch: 好了，快过去吧，别让星期日先生等急了。
t_en: Enough, get going. Don't keep Mr. Sunday waiting.

t_ch: …路呢？
t_en: ...A dead end?

t_ch: 大门紧闭，看来是要我们自己动手。
t_en: The door is shut tight. Looks like we're on our own.

t_ch: 我的意思是…这连门都没有。你之前怎么进去的？
t_en: I mean... there isn't a door. How did you get in before?

t_ch: 出于安全考量，家族将行政地点建在梦境深处，机关在这些隐夜鸫雕像里：<color=#dbc291ff>雕像的朝向是可以控制的</color>。
t_en: For security reasons, The Family built the administrative site deep in the Dreamscape, with the mechanisms hidden in these Nightingale statues. <color=#dbc291ff>The direction of the statues can be controlled</color>.

t_ch: 上一次，那位叫康娜的侍者专门去<color=#dbc291ff>侧面的房间</color>确认了什么，才把雕像布置到位。
t_en: On the previous occasion, an attendant named Conna had gone to <color=#dbc291ff>the side room</color> to verify something before setting the statues in the correct positions.

t_ch: 也许我们也得这么来一遍，去看看吧。当然，穷举也是个办法。
t_en: Maybe we should do the same. Let's go and take a look. Of course, we can also use brute force.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>六只朝向各不相同的隐夜鸫</color>，显而易见的提示。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Six Nightingales facing in different directions</color> — an obvious hint.

t_ch: 嗯…但这真的是隐夜鸫吗？
t_en: Hmm... but are these Nightingales?

t_ch: 是。怎么了？
t_en: They are. What's wrong?

t_ch: 哪有这么大的隐夜鸫？依我看，这应该是湮厄鹰。
t_en: How can Nightingales be so huge? They look more like Torment Eagles to me.

t_ch: 五大家系没有鹰，只有隐夜鸫——我为什么要跟你争这个？
t_en: There are no eagles in the Five Families, only Nightingales— Why am I wasting time with you on this?

t_ch: 果不其然，这就是正确答案。
t_en: Just as I thought, here's the correct answer.

t_ch: 真是伟大的发现——要不我把加入天才俱乐部的机会让给你吧。
t_en: A truly miraculous discovery — perhaps I should offer you the chance to join the Genius Society?

t_ch: 真的？我以为你早放下了呢。
t_en: Really? I thought you'd given up on that already.

t_ch: 这就是个该死的玩笑，你一点也听不出来吗？
t_en: I was being sarcastic. Can't you tell?

t_ch: 这里头够气派啊。看来星期日先生是完全不打算给自己树立清高的形象。
t_en: Impressive. So much for Mr Sunday's reserved, virtuous image.

t_ch: 要我提醒你么？这里是梦境，这座公馆的装潢再怎么豪华，也不会影响匹诺康尼一丝一毫。别在无聊的地方找家族的茬了。
t_en: Do you need me to remind you? We're in a dreamscape. No matter how grand the mansion looks, it'll not affect Penacony. Stop wasting your time nitpicking The Family here.

t_ch: 嗯，你说得对，家族的破绽只能是「死亡」，星期日肯定也是这么想的。咱们下楼吧。
t_en: Yeah, you're right. The only way to destroy The Family is "Death." Sunday must have thought the same. Let's head down.

t_ch: …等等。
t_en: ...Hold on.

t_ch: 怎么，走错路了？
t_en: What's wrong? Are we heading the wrong way?

t_ch: 没走错，但这扇门上锁了。
t_en: No, but this door is locked.

t_ch: 朋友，你真跟他约好了么？
t_en: My friend, did you really make an appointment with him?

t_ch: …这是个测试。在正式谈话前，你得先向星期日证明自己的价值。
t_en: ...It's a trial. You got to prove your worth to Sunday before you can speak with him.

t_ch: 如果我没猜错，我们得在这座大厅里找到开门的方法，否则此地就是你我的监牢。
t_en: If I'm not wrong, we need to find a way to open this door in the hall, or this place will be our prison.

t_ch: 哦，密室逃脱，我喜欢。
t_en: Oh, an escape room — my favorite.

t_ch: 严肃点，没人跟你玩。先回头吧，提示多半就在那座显眼的<color=#dbc291ff>沙盘</color>里。
t_en: Get serious. I've no time for games. Let's head back. The hint is probably in that prominent <color=#dbc291ff>sandpit</color>.

t_ch: 看这边，模型上有个非常明显的<color=#dbc291ff>缺口</color>，我大胆猜测你是对的。
t_en: Look, there's a noticeable <color=#dbc291ff>gap</color> in the model. I believe you're right.

t_ch: 上次来时还没这缺口，显然是那个男人有意布置。
t_en: There wasn't a gap before. That man must have done it intentionally.

t_ch: 那你天才般的大脑一定还记得这里原本放着什么吧，教授？
t_en: With your brilliant mind you shouldn't have any trouble recalling what was here last time, right, Doctor?

t_ch: 当然。
t_en: Of course.

t_ch: 将「格列佛拱门」置入插槽。
t_en: Insert Gulliver's Arch into the slot.

t_ch: 简直就像是搭积木。我从没搭过积木，不知道和垒筹码比，哪个更有意思……
t_en: This reminds me of one of those building toys. With the blocks? I've never played with them before. I wonder if it's more interesting than stacking chips.

t_ch: 看，严丝合缝，堪称完美——呃，接下来呢？
t_en: Look, the gap is closed, and it fits perfectly — eh, so what's next?

t_ch: 调查「格列佛拱门」。
t_en: Investigate Gulliver's Arch.

t_ch: 喔啊啊啊…！
t_en: Waaaaaah...!

t_ch: …天，我变小了？我这是在做梦吗？
t_en: ...Good heavens. Did I shrink? Am I still in a dream?

t_ch: 你的确是。
t_en: Indeed.

t_ch: 教授，你变得好高！我竟然来到了沙盒里，还变得这么小……
t_en: Doctor... you're huge! It's me! Down here in the sandpit!

t_ch: 要不咱们就这样离开？你再找个机会把我塞进星期日的衣领里，就用这种方式打入家族内部……
t_en: Actually I think we could make this work for us. Just find a way to slip me into Sunday's collar, and I'll infiltrate The Family just like that.

t_ch: 好吧，我就开个玩笑。
t_en: Fine. I was just kidding.

t_ch: 您好，欢迎来到「黄金的时刻」基底模型。
t_en: Hello. Welcome to the Golden Hour base model.

t_ch: 我是「橡木兵人」，负责引导您在基底模型的游览——乐意为您效劳。
t_en: I am an Oak Soldier. I will be here to guide you through the tour of the base model. Happy to be of service.

t_ch: 说说引导吧。
t_en: Tell me about the guided tour.

t_ch: 那就说说引导吧。
t_en: Then tell me about the tour.

t_ch: 您好好好好好，欢迎来到「黄金的时刻」基基基基基基底模型——
t_en: Hel—l—l—l—l—o. Welcome to the Golden Hour ba—a—a—a—a—se model —

t_ch: 我是「橡橡橡橡橡橡木兵人」，负责引导您在基底模型的游览览览览览览览览——
t_en: I am an O—o—o—o—o—oak Soldier. I will be here to guide you through the to—o—o—o—ur of the base mo —

t_ch: 我在问你话呢。
t_en: Hey, I was talking to you.

t_ch: 说说引导吧！
t_en: Tell me about the tour!

t_ch: （踢它一脚。）
t_en: (Give it a kick.)

t_ch: 览——乐意为您效劳。
t_en: Model. Happy to be of service.

t_ch: 正在在在在为您生成指引，请耐心等候候候候候……
t_en: Gen—n—n—n—n—n—nerating guide. Please wait pa—a—a—a—a—a—atient—t—t—t...

t_ch: 候…候…候…候…候……
t_en: t-t-t-t-t...

t_ch: 你真是世界上最烂的兵人。
t_en: You seriously are the worst soldier.

t_ch: 说，说，引，导，吧。
t_en: Tell. Me. About. The. Tour.

t_ch: （再踢它一脚。）
t_en: (Kick it again.)

t_ch: …已已已已已检索到最近的打卡点！
t_en: ...Fo—o—o—o—und the nearest check-in spot!

t_ch: 请看看看看看——我身后这台<color=#dbc291ff>扭蛋蛋蛋蛋蛋机</color>模型——
t_en: Please lo—o—o—o—ok behind me — a <color=#dbc291ff>capsule m—a—a—a—a—chine</color> model—

t_ch: 模型型型型型型型型型型型型型型——
t_en: Mode-e-e-e-e-e-e-l—

t_ch: <size=36>啊！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Ah!</size>

t_ch: 一声惨叫后，这个兵人崩溃了——甚至还没来得及让你为他打出五星好评。
t_en: After screaming, the soldier collapses — he didn't even have time to earn a five-star rating from you.

t_ch: 它崩溃了，可能需要一些时间重启。
t_en: It has crashed. It may take some time to reboot.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>扭蛋机</color>…地上没有能操作的地方，是头顶上那个？教授，帮我个小忙——
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Capsule machine</color>... There's no mechanism on the floor. Could there be one at the top? Doctor, do me a favor —

t_ch: 果然，这边只有个<color=#dbc291ff>弹珠机</color>的底座，空空如也。
t_en: Great. There's another <color=#dbc291ff>pinball machine</color> base here, and it's empty too.

t_ch: ——教授，又得仰仗你的智慧了。
t_en: ...Doc, I'll need your brainpower again.

t_ch: 不用大喊大叫，我听得见。<color=#dbc291ff>弹珠机</color>模型肯定和拱门一样放在大厅某个地方。等着，我马上回来。
t_en: There's no need to yell, I can hear you. The <color=#dbc291ff>pinball machine</color> must be hidden somewhere in the hall, like the arch. Wait here, and I'll be back in a minute.

t_ch: 很奇妙的装置。如果里面那个聒噪的小人消失，我对它的评价会更高。
t_en: Quite a fascinating contraption. I would rate it more favorably if that clamorous little person inside were gone.

t_ch: 拉帝奥——我听到了——
t_en: Ratio... I can hear you...

t_ch: 哈，你回来了——！把它放在这儿就行——感激不尽——！
t_en: Ha, you're back! Just place it here... Thank you...!

t_ch: 将模型置入沙盘。
t_en: Place the model in the sandpit.

t_ch: 很遗憾看到你活着离开沙盘，星期日就在这扇门后。以我粗浅的见解，他不是什么好对付的人，你准备好了么？
t_en: It's a pity you made it out of the sandpit alive. Sunday is just beyond this door. From my limited understanding, he's not someone easily handled. Are you prepared?

t_ch: 嗯，我更相信要做好准备的是他。
t_en: Yeah, only I believe he's the one who should be prepared to face me.

t_ch: 说说你的计划吧。
t_en: Tell me about your plan.

t_ch: 没什么计划，随机应变。与人交涉的筹码无非两种：利益或者恐惧。
t_en: I don't have a plan. I'll just play it by ear. There are only two kinds of bargaining chips when dealing with people: Benefit or fear.

t_ch: 看来你的确不理解「真诚」。
t_en: Looks like "sincerity" isn't in your dictionary.

t_ch: 我还不够真诚么？不用特意强调。我们要好好利用死亡，那男人的妹妹死了，他肯定坐不住的，这就是「恐惧」。
t_en: Am I not sincere enough? There's no need to emphasize it. We've got to make good use of death. That man's sister is dead. He won't be able to turn a blind eye, and that's "fear."

t_ch: 而我会帮他把那个杀人凶手揪出来。碍于身份和立场，他自己办不到这事，但我可以，这就是「利益」。
t_en: And I'll help him find the murderer. He can't do it due to his status and position, but I can, and that's "benefit."

t_ch: 你凭什么觉得他做不到，非得委托一个立场对立的公司人？
t_en: On what basis do you believe he's incapable, necessitating the delegation to someone from a rival faction, the IPC?

t_ch: 很简单——因为那凶手很可能是潜伏在家族中的*叛徒*。
t_en: Simple — Because that murderer could very well be a traitor hiding inside The Family.

t_ch: …你之前指控的可是那个巡海游侠。
t_en: ...Do you mean the Galaxy Ranger whom you accused previously?

t_ch: 那就是个借口，教授。那女人不对劲，我需要有人牵制她，在我们行动时视野外的变数越少越好。
t_en: That was just an excuse, good doctor. There's something wrong with that woman, and we need someone to keep her in check. It's better to minimize the variables outside of our control when we execute our plans.

t_ch: 我也需要知道她是什么人，如果我的好运货真价实，她一定能成为重要的棋子。而在这件事上能帮我的*朋友*，越多越好。
t_en: Moreover, I need to know her identity. If I'm lucky, she could be an important pawn. And it's good to have more helpful friends when dealing with this matter.

t_ch: 但说真心话，命案多半和她无关，我依旧是那个观点：肯定是家族内部出了问题。不然我们的星期日先生为何要安排私下会面？这不是一场审讯，而是一次秘密谈判。
t_en: But honestly, the murder case is likely unrelated to her. I believe my standpoint: There's a rat in The Family. Otherwise, why would Mr. Sunday arrange a private meeting with us? This isn't an interrogation, but a secret negotiation.

t_ch: 看着吧，以知更鸟的死为筹码，我会为自己赢回<color=#dbc291ff>自由和力量</color>。最后，我会颠覆这场美梦，创造<color=#dbc291ff>最盛大的死亡</color>。
t_en: We'll see. Using Robin's death as a bargaining chip, I'll win back my <color=#dbc291ff>freedom and power</color>. In the end, I'll ruin this beautiful dream and create <color=#dbc291ff>the grandest death</color>.

t_ch: 如果踏进这扇门就能迎来凯旋的机会，哪怕概率无限趋近于零，我也没有犹豫的理由，不是么？
t_en: If the chance of winning is just beyond this door, even if that chance is close to zero, well... you can't win if you don't play, right?

t_ch: 你凭什么觉得自己能赢，该死的赌徒？
t_en: Ah, the charming audacity! To think that you, of all people, might emerge victorious, dear gambler.

t_ch: 三枚「筹码」足矣。
t_en: Three chips are enough.

t_ch: 所有，或者一无所有。
t_en: All or nothing.

t_ch: 看来我布置的谜题对你还是太简单了，公司的使节。
t_en: It seems my puzzles are too effortless for you, IPC Ambassador.

t_ch: 承蒙谬赞，也感谢您花了这么多心思来欢迎我，星期日先生。只是这实在不像诚心邀约之人会做的事。
t_en: I appreciate your words, and I see you put a lot of effort into welcoming me, Mr. Sunday. However, this is no way to greet a guest.

t_ch: 所以这并非邀请，而是传唤，在谈话开始前，我需要对你的品性做些考验。
t_en: Well, this isn't an invitation, but a summoning. Before we speak, I need to test your character.

t_ch: 我猜，你身边这位博学的朋友帮了不少忙吧？
t_en: I imagine this knowledgeable doctor friend of yours has been of great help, yes?

t_ch: 当然，您应该比我更清楚这点——他已经忠实地履行了自己的职责，对吧？
t_en: Certainly, you ought to know this better than I do — he has already faithfully fulfilled his duties, hasn't he?

t_ch: 嗯，此前教授为你高贵的人格做了保证。他说你们二人的心地一样正直，是家族可以信赖的对象。
t_en: Yes, the doctor has assured me of your noble character. He considers you, like himself, a virtuous person who can be trusted by The Family.

t_ch: 我现在非常了解你的为人了，砂金先生。你勤勉、慷慨、乐于合作，又成功穿越重重阻碍来到我的面前——这令我有理由相信你的智慧与果敢。
t_en: I have come to know you very well as a person, Mr. Aventurine. You're diligent, generous, and willing to cooperate. The fact that you succeeded in overcoming many obstacles just to meet me gave me the reason to believe in your wisdom and courage.

t_ch: 但有一件事，我是要质问你的，那就是你的才智偏偏用错了地方，令你约见不该约见的人，出现在不该出现的场合…目睹了不应发生的{RUBY_B#知更鸟案}惨剧{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: But there's one thing I must ask you — that is, you've used your wisdom at the wrong place to meet the wrong person and put yourself in a situation where you shouldn't be... witnessing a {RUBY_B#Robin's Case}tragedy{RUBY_E#} that shouldn't have happened.

t_ch: 您的脸色看起来不太好啊。姑且确认一下，让您感到焦躁的是我吗？
t_en: You don't look too well. Am I making you anxious?

t_ch: …如果不是，那我就是站在您这边的。
t_en: ...If not, then it means I'm on your side.

t_ch: 如果我没理解错，你这番话…可是对*家族*提出了极其严重的指控。
t_en: If I wasn't mistaken, you'd just made a serious accusation against The Family.

t_ch: 您确实没理解错，因为邪恶正在您的身边悄然滋生。
t_en: You weren't mistaken, for depravity is creeping in around you.

t_ch: 我们不必遮遮掩掩，来谈谈您的妹妹吧。令妹的才能在演艺界无出其右，可您也知道，回到匹诺康尼后，她的声音就一直不太*谐调*。
t_en: There's no need for us to be evasive. Let's talk about your sister. Your sister's talent is unrivaled in the world of show business. As you know, her voice has been out of tune since she returned to Penacony. What's more disheartening, she {RUBY_B#Death}can't sing anymore{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 更可怕的是，她现在{RUBY_B#死}再也无法歌唱了{RUBY_E#}。谁做的？人们都觉得凶手在外来者中，但我知道…您心里另有答案。
t_en: Who could be responsible for this? Many suspect the culprit is among the outsiders, but I know... you hold a different opinion.

t_ch: 如今，您高贵的身份反成了镣铐，让您无法出手缉拿凶手，为令妹报仇雪恨。您孤立无援，才会感到焦躁不堪。
t_en: Now, your noble status has become a shackle, preventing you from apprehending the murderer and avenging your sister's death. You're feeling anxious because you're out on a limb.

t_ch: 但别担心，我是站在您这边的。
t_en: But don't worry. I am on your side.

t_ch: 砂金先生如此为我着想，是我莫大的荣幸——那么你这样无私慷慨的人，应该不会要求回报吧？
t_en: I'm immensely honored by your concern for me, Mr. Aventurine — since you're so selfless and generous, I believe you wouldn't ask for anything in return, would you?

t_ch: 当然，您不会因此损失什么，我只想取回本属于自己的东西：人身自由，还有家族保管下的随身物品——那袋礼金，还有……
t_en: Naturally, you wouldn't incur any loss from this. I just want to reclaim what is mine: My liberty, and the personal items under The Family's custody — the bag of gift money, and...

t_ch: 存放「基石」的匣子。
t_en: The box in which the Cornerstone is stored.

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: That's right.

t_ch: 「基石」——我听闻那是战略投资部的宝贵资产，封存「存护」令使大权的圣石，列位{RUBY_B#石心十人}清算专家{RUBY_E#}各自持有一枚。
t_en: Cornerstone — I've heard it's a treasured asset of the Strategic Investment Department, a sacred stone that seals the "Preservation" Emanator, granting significant power, and every {RUBY_B#Ten Stonehearts}Liquidation Specialist{RUBY_E#} holds one.

t_ch: …如此贵重的物品，恐怕只比其他回报更为昂贵。
t_en: ...For an object so precious, it probably comes at an even higher price than other forms of recompense.

t_ch: 但您也知道，若想真相水落石出，一点高昂的风险是必须的。
t_en: I'm sure you're aware of the high level of risk I'll be undertaking to bring the truth to light.

t_ch: 砂金先生，出门在外，你会时刻关注自己的仪容么？领带应在正中线上，衬衣不得从马甲中露出，裤线必须笔直，且始终对齐鞋头的朝向。
t_en: Mr. Aventurine, when you're out and about, do you always make adjustments to your appearance? Your tie should be on the center line, your shirt must not protrude from your vest, your trouser creases should be perfectly straight, and always aligned with the tips of your shoes.

t_ch: 当然会。
t_en: Of course.

t_ch: 但我不会，因为这不*得体*——你应当在出门前就确保一切井然有序，绝不偏移。
t_en: But I don't, because it's not appropriate to do so in public — you should make sure everything is presentable and in order before leaving the house.

t_ch: 我从不承担任何风险。基石必须由家族来保管。
t_en: I'm not the kind that takes risks. The Cornerstone must be in the custody of The Family.

t_ch: 真没得聊？
t_en: No room for negotiation?

t_ch: 别让我拒绝第二遍。
t_en: Please don't let me turn you down twice.

t_ch: …行吧，只拿回礼金也可以，这你总该给我了。一个商人如果没有交易的筹码，恐怕寸步难行啊。
t_en: ...Sure. The gift money is good enough. I suppose you wouldn't mind that? After all, a merchant can't function without a bargaining chip.

t_ch: 你的妥协比我预想中还要快些。可惜，比起商人…赌徒才更需要筹码。我可以给你礼金，但在这之前，我要你亲口告诉我——
t_en: You compromised quicker than I thought. Unfortunately, it's a gambler that needs a bargaining chip... not a merchant. I can give you your gift money, but before that, I want you to tell me —

t_ch: ——这个被你果断放弃的匣子里，究竟存放着什么？
t_en: — The fact that you can decisively forsake the box you asked for, what exactly is stored in it?

t_ch: 「三重面相的灵魂啊，请你用热铁烙他的舌和手心，使他不能编造谎话，立定假誓。」
t_en: "Oh, Triple-Faced Soul, please sear his tongue and palms with a hot iron, so that he will not be able to fabricate lies and make false vows."

t_ch: …你做了什么？
t_en: ...What have you done?

t_ch: 「同谐」的光照下，一切罪恶无所遁形，我恳请祂降下光芒，并代祂向你提问。接下来…你有<unbreak>113</unbreak>秒的时间自证清白，得到我的信任。
t_en: Under the light of the Harmony, all wickedness is revealed. I implore THEM to shed THEIR light, and I'll ask you questions on THEIR behalf. Next... you have <unbreak>113</unbreak> seconds to prove your innocence and gain my trust.

t_ch: 如果我拒绝回答呢？
t_en: And if I refuse to answer?

t_ch: 那就试试看——看「同谐」会不会拒绝你。
t_en: You can try — and we'll see if the Harmony rejects you.

t_ch: 「试问：你是否持有基石？」
t_en: "Question: Do you own a Cornerstone?"

t_ch: 是。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 很简洁的回答。你也明白言多必失的道理。
t_en: What a simple answer. You, too, understand that idle chatter leads only to poverty.

t_ch: 「你在入境时，是否将基石交予家族？」
t_en: "Did you hand over the Cornerstone to The Family when you entered Penacony?"

t_ch: 是。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 「你交予家族的基石是否属于你？」
t_en: "Does the Cornerstone you handed over to The Family belong to you?"

t_ch: 是。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 「此刻，你的基石是否就在这个房间里？」
t_en: "Is your Cornerstone in this room right now?"

t_ch: 是。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 「你的记忆是否没有遭到任何形式的篡改、删除，包括但不限于流光忆庭的技术。」
t_en: "Is your memory free from any kind of tampering or deletion, encompassing but not restricted to the techniques of the Garden of Recollection?"

t_ch: 是。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 「你是否来自茨冈尼亚的埃维金氏族？」
t_en: "Are you an Avgin from Sigonia?"

t_ch: 是。你连这个都知道？
t_en: Yes. You even know about that?

t_ch: 「埃维金人是否没有任何读取、篡改、操纵自己或他人思想的能力？」
t_en: "Do the Avgins have any ability to read, tamper with, or manipulate one's own or another's mind?"

t_ch: 没有，这有关系吗？
t_en: No. Does it matter?

t_ch: 「你爱家人胜过爱你自己吗？」
t_en: "Do you love your family more than yourself?"

t_ch: 是。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 「所有埃维金人都在一场屠杀中丧命了，是吗？」
t_en: "All the Avgins were killed in a massacre. Am I right?"

t_ch: 不是。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 「你是氏族中唯一的幸存者吗？」
t_en: "Are you your clan's sole survivor?"

t_ch: …也许吧。
t_en: ...Perhaps.

t_ch: 「——你憎恨，并想要亲手毁灭这个世界吗？」
t_en: "...Do you hate and wish to destroy this world with your own hands?"

t_ch: 我不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 有趣。那么最后一个问题……
t_en: Interesting. Now, the final question...

t_ch: 「你是否能够立誓，此刻——『砂金石』正安然无恙地躺在这个匣子里？」
t_en: "Can you swear that at this very moment, the Aventurine stone is safe and sound in this box?"

t_ch: …当然。
t_en: ...Of course.

t_ch: 看来我们能得出答案了。
t_en: Looks like we can get an answer.

t_ch: 打开它吧，砂金先生…这是你维护自己名誉的最后机会。
t_en: Open it, Mr. Aventurine... It's your last chance to defend your honor.

t_ch: 既然您如期赴约，博学的教授…这是否意味着，您愿意在这场闹剧中站在家族这边？
t_en: Since you came as promised, learned Doctor... Does this mean that you are willing to take the side of The Family in this farce?

t_ch: 是什么让你觉得自己可以拉拢我？
t_en: What makes you think you can convince me?

t_ch: 我已有所耳闻，您与砂金先生的相处并不愉快。我也知道您是一位真正的学者，对知识的追求大过其他一切。
t_en: I've heard you haven't enjoyed Mr. Aventurine's company. I also understand that you're an avid learner who sees the pursuit of knowledge above all.

t_ch: 那你应该也明白，一位合格的学者能认清自己的位置，不会为无聊的尊严丢失更重要的东西。
t_en: In that case, you ought to realize that a competent scholar knows their position and wouldn't forsake more vital matters for the sake of petty pride.

t_ch: 若您同意协助家族，我会把我们对星核的研究成果如数奉上。您应该很清楚，除了家族，没有任何派系愿意分享这样的知识。
t_en: If you agree to assist The Family, I'll share our research findings on the Stellaron. You must be quite aware that, besides The Family, no other faction is willing to share such information.

t_ch: 说说看吧，需要我做什么？
t_en: Cut to the chase. What do you need from me?

t_ch: 我需要砂金先生的全盘计划。
t_en: I need Mr. Aventurine's comprehensive plan.

t_ch: 你们不都把他的「基石」扣下了？拔光了毛的孔雀可飞不起来。
t_en: Haven't you confiscated his Cornerstone? You can't expect a featherless bird to take flight.

t_ch: 但我也听闻战略投资部的{RUBY_B#石心十人}十位精英{RUBY_E#}极为团结，在公司利益面前共同进退。
t_en: But I've also heard the {RUBY_B#Ten Stonehearts}ten elites{RUBY_E#} in the Strategic Investment Department have united, progressing together in the interests of the IPC.

t_ch: 你可以把话说的更明白些。
t_en: You'll have to speak more clearly than that.

t_ch: 砂金先生呈交的「基石」——当真属于他本人么？
t_en: The Cornerstone which Mr. Aventurine surrendered — was it really his?

t_ch: 你怀疑他会把别人的基石交给你？你把石心十人想的太团结了——那玩意可比他们的命重要得多。
t_en: You question whether he would entrust you with someone else's Cornerstone? The Ten Stonehearts aren't as united as you think — Cornerstones are significantly more precious to them than their very own lives.

t_ch: 但您也知道他是个疯狂的赌徒。愈是声势张扬，愈要细心提防。
t_en: But you know that he's a crazed gambler. The more vocal he is about it, the more cautious I must be.

t_ch: 我从没想过会有人和他思路一致。说真的，你最好去看看心理医生。
t_en: I never imagined someone would share his way of thinking. Honestly, you should see a shrink.

t_ch: 拿来吧。存放基石的匣子是特制的，除公司高层及相关人员外，没人能打开它——但我恰好位列其中。
t_en: Bring it. The box containing Cornerstone is unique, and only IPC senior staff and related members can access it — but I happen to be among them.

t_ch: 劳驾。
t_en: I appreciate it.

t_ch: ——很遗憾，你猜对了。
t_en: Unfortunately, your guess is correct.

t_ch: 呵，金黄色的石头啊。它的色泽和克里珀圣体的光芒如出一辙。
t_en: Ha, the golden stone. Its color and glow are similar to that of Qlipoth's body.

t_ch: 这正是他准备用来欺瞒你的说辞。他不会告诉你：石心十人需以自身意志开凿基石，令其绽放独一无二的光辉。
t_en: This is the very ruse he intends to use to fool you. He won't reveal to you that the Ten Stonehearts chisel their own will into the Cornerstones, granting them an unparalleled radiance.

t_ch: 而这颗金黄色的石头属于{RUBY_B#「石心十人」成员}托帕{RUBY_E#}，它的别名是「黄玉」——不是「砂金」。
t_en: And this golden stone is also known as a topaz, not aventurine, and it belongs to {RUBY_B#One of the Ten Stonehearts}Topaz{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 如何，要找他对质么？
t_en: So? Do you wish to confront him?

t_ch: 暂且不必。我现在更希望知道，属于他的那枚基石在哪。
t_en: Not at the moment. I'm more interested to know the location of his Cornerstone.

t_ch: 最安全的场所，你绝对想不到的地方。因为他根本就没打算藏起来——从最开始，那颗基石就已经在你的手里了。
t_en: The safest place, somewhere you'd never think of. Because he never intended to hide it — in fact, that Cornerstone has been in your hands from the very beginning.

t_ch: 原来如此，这行李袋…将比性命更珍贵的基石混在一堆低贱的珠宝里，伪装成礼金等待被扣押，倒确实符合那位{RUBY_B#赌徒}砂金先生{RUBY_E#}的风格。
t_en: I see, this bag... Mixing a Cornerstone, more precious than life itself, with a bunch of worthless jewels, disguised as a gift of money waiting to be confiscated, is indeed in line with {RUBY_B#Gambler}Mr. Aventurine{RUBY_E#}'s style.

t_ch: 然后再随便编个理由，避重就轻，找你把礼金要回来。这是场赌局，他可太熟悉了，赌的就是你一着不慎，满盘皆输。
t_en: Then he makes up some trivial excuse, downplaying the matter, and requests the gift money. This is a gamble, one he's all too familiar with, betting on your single misstep leading to a total loss.

t_ch: 博学的教授，感谢您的帮助。家族自会答谢义人。
t_en: Learned Doctor, I'm grateful for your help. The Family will surely reward a righteous person like you.

t_ch: 至于恶徒…愿他退后受辱。
t_en: As for the villain... I hope he retreats in humiliation.

t_ch: 多亏你有一位眼光独到的朋友，我才能为你的职业生涯添上一次彻底的失败。
t_en: It was all thanks to your friend with a keen eye that I could add a blot of utter failure to your storied career.

t_ch: 拉帝奥，你这混蛋……
t_en: Ratio, you wretch...

t_ch: 原形毕露了啊。顺便一提，你的生命*暂时*只剩下十七个系统时了。珍惜这段时间，好好回味失败的余韵吧。
t_en: Finally shown your true colors, heh. Oh, and just to remind you: You currently only have seventeen system hours left to live. Treasure your remaining time, and savor the delectable aftertaste of defeat.

t_ch: …你不妨把话说得更明白些。
t_en: ...You might as well explain yourself a little more clearly.

t_ch: 我方才为你施行的，是「同谐」的圣洗。你本应在祂的光照下展现忠诚，却一意孤行，满口谎言，将洗礼变作了审判。我实在没有理由为你解开它。
t_en: What I performed on you just now was the Harmony's consecration. You were to show allegiance beneath the illumination of THEIR grace, yet you acted willfully, uttering nothing but falsehoods, transforming the consecration into a trial. I genuinely see no reason to absolve you from it.

t_ch: 这就是所谓的「同谐」？…建立在拘禁和逼迫之上？
t_en: Is this what the Harmony represents? Is it built upon constraint and coercion?

t_ch: 你误会了，砂金先生。刑罚是为亵慢之人准备的，但我看到了你坚韧不拔的内心，因此要赐你新生的可能。
t_en: You misunderstand, Mr. Aventurine. Punishment is meant for the irreverent, but I have seen your resilient spirit, and thus I offer you the possibility of a new beginning.

t_ch: 这十七个系统时里，你无法离开梦境，也无法与任何同伴往来。你只有两条路可走，这取决于约定的时限内，你能否完成我的考验。
t_en: Throughout these seventeen system hours, you will be unable to escape the Dreamscape or contact any of your companions. You only have two paths before you, and it all depends on whether you can complete my test within the time limit.

t_ch: 若成功，你便能融入谐乐，与万千家人同在；若失败，则将承受{RUBY_B#「同谐」的化身}「无限夫长」{RUBY_E#}的怒火，坠入万劫不复的深渊。所以我才特意强调了*暂时*二字。
t_en: Should you succeed, you will be able to coalesce into the Harmony and be with your Family. If you fail, you will suffer the wrath of the {RUBY_B#Embodiment of Harmony}Eternal Centurion{RUBY_E#} and fall into an abyss of doom.

t_ch: …该死的，听起来我的下场横竖都一样啊。
t_en: Sounds like I'm going to end up the same either way.

t_ch: 我确实需要一位仆人，助我从外部找出家族中潜伏的*邪恶*。而我会自内向外肃清，在十七个系统时内将真凶捉拿归案。
t_en: I indeed do need a servant to help me uncover the evil hidden in The Family from an external perspective. I will purge the evil from the inside and bring the real culprits to justice within seventeen system hours.

t_ch: 等时候到了，就将你的发现同我核验。如果我们二人的判断一致，或者你能带给我更多…那祂便能将慈爱和诚实真正地施给你了。
t_en: When the time comes, compare your findings with mine. If both our findings align, or if you can provide me more insights... then THEY will truly be able to grant you mercy and honesty.

t_ch: …无耻的伪君子，你没收了我所有的东西，还要我给你真相？这不公平，在你们这座充满铜臭味的游乐园里，没钱办不成任何事。
t_en: Shameless hypocrites... You took everything from me and still demand the truth? That isn't fair. Your carnival reeks with the stench of cash. Nothing is achievable without it!

t_ch: 这应当是你个人的义举，无需家族的援助。你的行李袋在那里，请便吧，相信你能用这袋低贱的珠宝换来一切。这是赌徒最擅长的事，不是么？
t_en: This is meant to be an act of personal virtue, not requiring The Family's support. Your bag is over there, do as you please. I believe you can trade this bag of worthless jewels for everything you need. That's what gamblers excel at, isn't it?

t_ch: 出发吧，砂金先生，你*自由*了。我会在这里等你报喜。
t_en: Off you go, Mr. Aventurine. You are free. I will wait here for your good news.

t_ch: 所以，这次会面不是审讯，但也根本不是什么谈判……
t_en: This meeting isn't an interrogation, or a negotiation...

t_ch: …而是一场彻头彻尾的私刑，对吗？
t_en: It's an outright execution.

t_ch: 怎么会，砂金先生，我真的只是想知道，一位*偶然*出现在她命案现场的*过客*能有什么*发现*，仅此而已。
t_en: Why would I do that, Mr. Aventurine? I'm just wondering what a passerby who stumbled upon a scene of murder could have found out, that's all.

t_ch: 对了，在你临走前，我还有个比较私人的问题。
t_en: By the way, before you go, I have a personal question.

t_ch: 又怎么了？
t_en: What is it now?
You...

t_ch: …真的想要亲手毁灭这个世界吗？
t_en: ...Do you truly wish to bring about the destruction of this world?

t_ch: 铭牌上写着：<color=#dbc291ff>格列佛拱门</color>。这么个小玩意儿你都能记得一清二楚？这东西的外观让我想起一种通过就能使人缩小的隧……
t_en: The nameplate reads: <color=#dbc291ff>Gulliver's Arch</color>. I'm amazed you can remember something this tiny. You know, this reminds me of a tunnel I once saw that could shrink people who passed through it.

t_ch: 如果我是你，就会立刻闭嘴。不该说的话少说。
t_en: If I were you, I would shut my mouth. It's wise to remain silent when you should.

t_ch: 还真是这样。这些模型连内部构造都和真实建筑一模一样，区别只是没人居住。
t_en: So I was right. These models have interiors that look exactly like the real buildings. The only difference is that no one lives in them.

t_ch: 星期日把这么个微缩模型摆在每天起床就能看见的地方，他真把自己当成匹诺康尼的*巨人*了？
t_en: Funny that Sunday puts a miniature that makes him seem like a giant by comparison right where he can see it first thing in the morning. Insecure much?

t_ch: 教授——你怎么还没走——？
t_en: Doctor... Shake a leg...?

t_ch: 没有那个<color=#dbc291ff>弹珠机</color>模型——我们都得交待在这儿——！
t_en: Without that <color=#dbc291ff>Pinball machine</color>, neither one of us is going anywhere anytime soon.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼也不都是坏事，对吧？我会把这段有趣经历当作牌桌上的谈资的。
t_en: Penacony isn't all bad, right? I'll use this interesting experience as a talking point at the poker table.

t_ch: 喏，我就说——穷举也能解决问题。
t_en: Look, like I said — brute force can also solve things.

t_ch: 「庸众院」真该给你留一席，全宇宙没人比你更配得上这个名字。
t_en: The Council of Mundanites should consider you as their member, for no one in this universe is more suitable to be there than you.

t_ch: 用穷举法解题并不能证明你有多高明。<color=#dbc291ff>侧面的房间</color>，别让我强调第二次。
t_en: Solving a problem by brute force doesn't prove your intelligence. <color=#dbc291ff>The side room</color> — don't let me repeat myself.

t_ch: 哟，好大的沙盘…等存够钱，我也想给自己盖栋这么高的楼。
t_en: Wow, that's an enormous sandpit... I'd love to build a tall building for myself once I have enough savings.

t_ch: 四下搜查一遍，只要看见，我就能认出来。
t_en: Let's look around. When I see it, I will know it.

t_ch: 好，好，我怎么感觉咱们这是在攻读入室盗窃学的学位呢？
t_en: Fine, fine. Why do I feel that we're pursuing a degree in burglary now?

t_ch: 找到了，就是它。
t_en: Found it. This is it.

t_ch: 咱们还是找找开门的办法吧。
t_en: Let's find a way to open the door.

t_ch: 什么情况，家族的玩具都会碰瓷？我可什么都没做啊，教授，你要为我作证。
t_en: What's up with that? Now The Family's toys are trying to frame me? I didn't do a thing to it, Doc. You've gotta be my witness.

t_ch: 我没看见。
t_en: I saw nothing.

t_ch: 还有一枚碎片飞到楼上去了，得通过<color=#dbc291ff>弹珠机</color>蹦到那里。但多半没这么容易。
t_en: One of the fragments flew upstairs. I'll need to use the <color=#dbc291ff>pinball machine</color> to flick myself up there, but it's tough.

t_ch: 总算能安静一会儿了。
t_en: Finally, a moment of peace.

t_ch: 就是它了…愉快的独处时光总是转瞬即逝。
t_en: That's it... Pleasant moments of solitude are always fleeting.

t_ch: 多谢了，教授！
t_en: Thanks, Doc!

t_ch: 拉帝奥，你真该进来看看，太壮观了，我敢说你轻轻一捏就能把我弄死。
t_en: Ratio, you should come in and take a look. The view here is breathtaking. Honestly, you could easily squash me with just a pinch.

t_ch: 只要本人同意，我立即执行。
t_en: If that is your wish, I will do so without a moment's hesitation.

t_ch: 全部搞定，轻轻松松。
t_en: All done. Piece of cake.

t_ch: 愉快的玩具城之旅结束了，还有点舍不得呢。
t_en: And the joyous tour of Toy City has come to an end. It makes me feel sad.

t_ch: 不好意思……
t_en: Umm, excuse me...

t_ch: 鸢尾花家系的档案中…似乎找不到这位艺者的信息，您提供的照片也无法匹配……
t_en: I can't seem to find any information on this artist in the Iris Family archives. The photo you provided also doesn't show any matches...

t_ch: …这也在意料中。我想再请教下，人们入梦时通常会留下什么痕迹么？
t_en: ...Just as I thought. I'd like to ask, what kind of traces do people leave when they enter a dream?

t_ch: 您是指入梦池的记录么？设备会实时监测心率、血氧含量、体温这些生理指标，并列入统计，交由家族筛查其中的异常数据，一旦发现非法行为，就要立即采取措施。
t_en: Are you referring to the records when you enter the Dreampool? The equipment will monitor physiological indicators such as heart rate, blood oxygen levels, and body temperature in real time. This data will be included in statistics and handed over to The Family for the screening of any data anomalies. Immediate action will be taken once any illegal behavior is detected.

t_ch: 可以调取这些记录吗？
t_en: Is it possible to access these records?

t_ch: 感觉一举一动都在被监视…
t_en: Feels like our every move is being watched...

t_ch: 请放心。监控各位的生命体征是为了在紧急情况下进行强制唤醒，一切出发点都是客人们的安全。
t_en: Please rest assured. Your vitals are monitored in case of emergencies, allowing us to wake you if necessary. This is all for the safety of our guests.

t_ch: 能麻烦您调取这些记录吗？
t_en: Can I trouble you to access the records?

t_ch: 抱歉…酒店没有这个权限。这些信息由猎犬家系统一管理，只有出现具体问题时，才会转到这边。
t_en: I apologize... The hotel does not have access. This information is managed by the Bloodhound Family. We can only gain access if there's a problem.

t_ch: 看来这里是查不出什么了。
t_en: Looks like nothing can be found here.

t_ch: 至少我们知道了下一步该找谁，顺便可以向猎犬家系打听下案情。
t_en: At least we know who to look for next. We can ask the Bloodhound Family for information.

t_ch: 感谢您的协助，艾丽小姐。对了…知更鸟小姐还好吧？我们都很期待她的表演。
t_en: Thank you for your assistance, Miss Alley. By the way... is Miss Robin doing fine? We are looking forward to her performance.

t_ch: 「还好」…是什么意思？知更鸟小姐怎么了吗，谐乐大典的筹备工作一直在有序进行，应该挺好的吧，相信她能为各位带来最精彩的演出。
t_en: "Fine"... What does that mean? Is there something wrong with Miss Robin? The preparations for the Charmony Festival have been proceeding smoothly, so I guess things are pretty good. I believe she will be able to put on a spectacular show for all of you.

t_ch: 嗯，一定会。
t_en: Mm, I'm sure.

t_ch: 果然没人知道知更鸟的事，倒不意外。
t_en: Sure enough, no one knows about Miss Robin. I'm not surprised.

t_ch: 但那位流萤小姐也真是神秘，酒店系统中竟然找不到任何有关她的信息。就算是偷渡犯…入境后也该有个伪装的身份吧。
t_en: But that Miss Firefly is truly mysterious. There's no information on her in the hotel system. Even if she's a stowaway... she should have a disguised identity after entering the planet.

t_ch: 再加上她同为遗产争夺战的参与者…要如何在神不知鬼不觉的情况下潜入梦境？
t_en: She's also in the running for the Legacy... How is she going to sneak into the Dreamscape unnoticed?

t_ch: 除了酒店客房的入梦池，还有其他入梦方法么？
t_en: Is there any other way to enter a dream besides the hotel room's Dreampool?

t_ch: 流光忆庭…
t_en: The Garden of Recollection...

t_ch: 星核猎手…
t_en: Stellaron Hunters...

t_ch: 忆者拥有常人难以理解的能力，在匹诺康尼的忆域中可谓如鱼得水，黑天鹅已经向我们证明了这点。
t_en: The Memokeepers have abilities that are difficult for normal people to comprehend. In the Memory Zone of Penacony, they thrive effortlessly, a fact proven to us by Black Swan.

t_ch: 而星核猎手的骇客小姑娘用非常手段解开了梦境酒店的封锁，且根据{NICKNAME}看到的现场，流萤小姐一案背后是他们在推动。
t_en: The hacker girl from the Stellaron Hunters used extraordinary means to unlock the dreamscape Hotel's seal. According to the scene witnessed by {NICKNAME}, it is likely that they are behind Miss Firefly's case.

t_ch: 流光忆庭…还有星核猎手，确实都有可能呢…那公司呢？他们想得到匹诺康尼，肯定也会有所准备吧。
t_en: The Garden of Recollection... and the Stellaron Hunters, both are possible. But what about the IPC? Since they want Penacony all for themselves, they're bound to have a plan.

t_ch: 你、你们是什么人？
t_en: Wh—Who are you guys?

t_ch: 布拉沃工作小组已抵达指定位置，准备执行武装疏散作业——小的们，都给我动起来！
t_en: The Bravo team has arrived at their designated position. Ready to execute armed evacuation operation — boys, get moving!

t_ch: 武、武装疏散？老大，你是不是喝醉了……
t_en: Ar—armed evacuation? Boss, are you drunk?

t_ch: 你懂个屁，这样效率才高，别让总监发现就行了！先斩后奏，懂不懂？
t_en: What do you know!? It's more efficient this way. Just don't let the director find out! Act first, report later, understood?

t_ch: 救、救命…我的年终奖金都在那颗雪球上交代完了…我可不想自己的名字上部门的重大违纪通告啊！
t_en: Help... Help me... I spent all my year-end bonus on the snowball... I don't want my name on the department's major disciplinary notice!

t_ch: 喂，{NICKNAME}，你看！这声音…是贝洛伯格那帮公司员工？
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}, check it out! That voice... Could it be the IPC Workers from Belobog?

t_ch: 敬告各位住客：星际和平公司将在酒店开展特殊工作，请跟随负责疏散的员工前往指定安全区域，否则将被采取强制措施！
t_en: To all guests: The IPC will be conducting special operations within the hotel. Please follow the staff in charge of evacuation to the designated safe zones, or compulsory measures will be enforced!

t_ch: ——请你个大头鬼！这群记吃不记打的家伙，都说了工作时间不要乱喝东西！
t_en: I'll request a beating for you lot! You've been told not to drink during work hours!

t_ch: 你们几个，赶紧把他拖下去，送回客房——
t_en: Take him back to the hotel room!

t_ch: ——晚点我拉个会议，好好复盘下事故报告该怎么写！
t_en: ...I'll organize a meeting later to properly go over how this incident report should be written!

t_ch: （那两个人，看着有点眼熟……）
t_en: (Those two people look a little familiar...)

t_ch: 真是戒备森严…想必他们也在为案件发愁吧。
t_en: Such tight security... I bet they're stumped by the case as well.

t_ch: 加拉赫…加拉赫…他会在哪儿呢？
t_en: Gallagher... Gallagher... Where could he be?

t_ch: …托帕小姐！没想到会在匹诺康尼遇见你。
t_en: ...Miss Topaz! I never thought I'd run into you on Penacony.

t_ch: 好久不见，星穹列车的各位，你们的事我从砂金那里都听说啦…嗯？
t_en: Long time no see, Astral Express crew. Aventurine has told me a lot about your happenings... Hmm?

t_ch: …没关系，就按他们要求的做，尽量避免和家族起冲突…采取任何行动前先和我汇报。嗯。嗯，好。
t_en: ...It's fine. Do as they ask, and try to avoid any conflicts with The Family... Report to me before taking any action. Mhm. Yes, alright.

t_ch: 唉…如你们所见，公司在匹诺康尼可是不太受欢迎。家族的地主之谊也只是表面客气，曾经的边陲监狱，如今要反过来给公司职员戴上镣铐啦。
t_en: ...As you see — the IPC isn't very popular here on Penacony. Cordiality from The Family is a mere facade. The former frontier prison has turned around and cuffed its shackles on the IPC staff now.

t_ch: 也只有带着「邀请函」的砂金被允许参加盛会，我们这些随行人员…连入梦资格都没有，只能在现实酒店停留。
t_en: Only Aventurine, who carries an invitation is allowed to attend the banquet. An entourage like us... We can only sit around in the reality Hotel, unauthorized to even enter dreams.

t_ch: 难怪砂金到处找人合作，原来他在梦境里得不到公司的援助。
t_en: No wonder Aventurine's scrambling to partner up with someone — the IPC can't back him up in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 听说他处境不太乐观，你们在帮忙调查一些…对家族不利的事，对吧？在梦境外边，有什么需要随时和我说，公司向来不会亏待合作伙伴。
t_en: His situation isn't optimistic, I hear. You're all helping to investigate some... dirt on The Family, are you not? Let me know if you need anything outside the Dreamscape. The IPC always treats its partners well.

t_ch: 谢谢你，托帕小姐。我们正要找猎犬们打听案情，也许你已经和他们打过交道了？
t_en: Thank you, Miss Topaz. We're on our way to the Hounds to verify some intel. Perhaps you've had dealings with them?

t_ch: 喏，就在后边跟着呢，找他们就行——正好，帮我转移下视线，一直被人盯着太难受啦。
t_en: Yep, they're tailing us right now. Why not go and talk to them... it'll take the spotlight off me, being constantly stared at is really uncomfortable.

t_ch: 和砂金做生意的感觉怎么样，一定不太习惯吧？
t_en: How does it feel to be in business with Aventurine? I bet you're not used to it.

t_ch: 那人的风格就这样，「所有，或者一无所有」是他的老口头禅了。他总是一边套近乎，一边推着客户陪他铤而走险…人各有志，我不多评价。
t_en: That's just his style. "All or nothing" is his mantra. He's always cozying up to his clients while egging them on to undertake some dangerous assignment with him... But everyone has their merits, so I won't comment further.

t_ch: 不过砂金的运气确实很好，经手的案子大多圆满落幕，基本没赌输过。所以在收复匹诺康尼这件事上…我也拭目以待。
t_en: But Aventurine's luck has always been good. He's always closed all his cases without a hitch, and basically never lost a gamble. Which is why on the issue of retaking Penacony I'm watching with keen interest.

t_ch: 这么厉害啊…
t_en: He's that good huh...

t_ch: 前提是利益一致。
t_en: It works only when all parties' interests are aligned.

t_ch: 当然。交易嘛，重要的是你坐在桌子哪边。
t_en: Of course. It's business, after all. What's important is where you're seated at the table.

t_ch: 至于两起案件…抱歉，我手上的信息也不多。详情只能拜托各位继续调查了。
t_en: As for the two cases... Apologies, but I don't have much info on them either. All I can do is ask you to keep digging for more details.

t_ch: 拜托各位继续调查案情了，我在现实中等待你们的好消息。
t_en: Please continue to investigate the case. All I can do is sit tight in the real world. I look forward to hearing your progress.

t_ch: 我们在执行长官的命令！你、你有什么事！
t_en: We're carrying out our Captain's orders! Wh—What do you want!

t_ch: 我们之前犯了错，正在努力将功补过，没空管别的！监视公司高管托帕，确保她在匹诺康尼期间始终待在白日梦酒店——这次没找错人！
t_en: We made a mistake last time, and we're working hard to rectify it now. We don't have time for anything else! Surveilling the IPC Executive Topaz, ensuring that she stays put at The Reverie hotel during her time on Penacony... We've got the right one this time!

t_ch: （…原来如此，是当时追捕流萤的那两个人啊。）
t_en: (...So that's it, they were the pair who were after Firefly at the time.)

t_ch: 还记得我吗？
t_en: Remember me?

t_ch: 我们又见面了。
t_en: We meet again.

t_ch: 怎、怎么又是你…又想来找茬？这次我们可不怕你！
t_en: I—It's you again... Back for more trouble? We're not afraid of you this time!

t_ch: 有话快说，没事就别来妨碍我们！
t_en: Well? Spit it out! Stop bothering us if you've nothing important!

t_ch: 原来你们认识…{NICKNAME}，你怎么到哪都能遇见揍过的人啊？
t_en: So you know each other... {NICKNAME}, why do you keep running into people you've beaten up before?

t_ch: 我找你们长官有事，他在哪里？
t_en: I've got business with your Captain. Where is he?

t_ch: 没错，我们正在为家族调查凶案呢。可以找你们的长官聊聊案情吗？
t_en: That's right. We're investigating a murder for The Family. Can we speak to your Captain about the case?

t_ch: 我想请你们调些记录，办案用。
t_en: I need you to pull some records for me. It's for official business.

t_ch: 没错，我们正在为家族调查凶案呢。可以请你们调取酒店入梦池的记录看看吗？
t_en: That's right. We're investigating a murder for The Family. May we access the hotel's Dreampool entry records?

t_ch: 您吃了吗？吃的什么？
t_en: When's our lunch break? Mmm... Hungwy.

t_ch: 哎呀…你怎么老喜欢问这个？我们正在为家族调查凶案呢。可以找你们的长官聊聊案情吗？
t_en: Sheesh, why do you keep asking me that? We're in the middle of investigating a murder case for The Family. May we speak to your Captain about it?

t_ch: 这个……
t_en: Well...

t_ch: …喂！治安官交代过，他从筑梦边境回来前，不准透露任何消息！
t_en: ...Hey! The Security Officer instructed everyone to shut their traps before he returned from Dream's Edge!

t_ch: 什、什么凶案？你们可别乱说。
t_en: Wh—What murder? You'd better stop spouting nonsense.

t_ch: 对、对！我们无可奉告，请回吧！
t_en: Th—That's right! We have nothing to report. Please leave!

t_ch: 看来是不愿配合啊。不过，他们直接把长官在筑梦边境的事交代了……
t_en: Looks like they're not going to cooperate. But, they did at least tell us that the Captain is at Dream's Edge...

t_ch: 那我们直接去找那位治安官不就好了…应该就是{NICKNAME}提到的加拉赫吧？
t_en: Why don't we just look for the Security Officer then... It's probably Gallagher, the one {NICKNAME} mentioned, right?

t_ch: 我、我们无可奉告，请回吧。
t_en: W—We have nothing to report. Please leave.

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 两位，劳驾。猎犬家系正在前方调查，闲人免进。
t_en: Apologies. The Bloodhound Family is running an investigation up ahead. No unauthorized personnel allowed.

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: …等等，我好像见过你，灰头发的。
t_en: ...Hold on a minute. I think I've seen you before, the gray-haired one.

t_ch: …你在匹诺康尼到底惹了多少麻烦？
t_en: ...How much trouble have you stirred up exactly on Penacony?

t_ch: 你认错人了…吧。
t_en: This ain't it, chief. You've got the wrong {F#gal}{M#guy}.

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 我没认错！上次就是你，一边喊着友情努力钟表把戏，一边和那个银发小姑娘一起把我胖揍了一顿！
t_en: Not possible! It was you the last time, yelling about some clockwork friendship while beating me up with that silver-haired girl!

t_ch: 没错！就是我，钟表小子！
t_en: That's right! It's-a-me, Clockie!

t_ch: …你觉得自己长得像吗？
t_en: ...You think you look alike?

t_ch: 不然我给你表演个钟表把戏？
t_en: How about a clockwork trick?

t_ch: …不是，你还来真的啊？
t_en: ...Wait, are you for real?

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 我没认错！上次就是你，一边喊着友情努力钟表把戏，一边和那个银发小姑娘一起把我胖揍了一顿！
t_en: Not possible! It was you the last time, yelling about some clockwork friendship while beating me up with that silver-haired girl!

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 这次我说什么也不能放你过去了…请回吧，不然我可要跪下来求你了！
t_en: I'm not letting you get by this time... Please leave, or I'll have to get on my knees and beg you!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你究竟做了多么伤天害理的事啊……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, what kind of heinous crime have you committed now...

t_ch: 等等，这位先生…我们有家族授予的文书，会协助各位进行调查，可否行个方便，让我们见见那位加拉赫先生？
t_en: Hold on, Sir... We have documents authorized by The Family that would aid your investigation. If it wouldn't trouble you, could we see this Mr. Gallagher?

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 你们说的这加拉赫到底是谁？已经有好几个人跟我提起这名字了，这灰头发的上次也说过。
t_en: Who exactly is this Gallagher you keep talking about? There have been a few people mentioning this name, even the one with the gray hair.

t_ch: 啊？不是他派你们来这儿的吗？
t_en: Uh? He didn't send you all here?

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 是治安官大人派我们来的，别的我可不能多说了。
t_en: It was the Security Officer who dispatched us. That's all I can divulge.

t_ch: 这不就结了嘛，我们要找的就是他呀！
t_en: He'll do! He's the one we've been looking for!

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 真不行。老大说了，事关家族颜面，谁也不能放过去。
t_en: Sorry, no can do. The boss said that since it's a matter of The Family's reputation, no one's allowed through.

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 请回吧，各位，我们真没必要这样为难彼此，对吧？
t_en: Everyone, please leave. There's really no need for us to make things difficult for each other, right?

t_ch: 很抱歉给您带来困扰。我们走吧，{NICKNAME}、小三月，再想想其他办法。
t_en: We're really sorry for troubling you. {NICKNAME}, March, let's think of another way.

t_ch: 其他办法…对了！{NICKNAME}不是说，上次是靠那什么…钟表把戏，让这位大哥回心转意的嘛。
t_en: Another way... That's it! Didn't {NICKNAME} say something about that... What was it... clockwork? That got this guy to change his mind.

t_ch: 你能再表演一次吗，{NICKNAME}？就是那个——钟表小子的神奇魔术！
t_en: Can you perform it again, {NICKNAME}? That... Clockie magic!

t_ch: 就等你这句话呢！
t_en: Just what I've been waiting for!

t_ch: 可不能做坏事啊。
t_en: But I can't abuse it for evildoings.

t_ch: 只是要解开误会，不算做坏事吧！
t_en: It's not bad if we're resolving a misunderstanding!

t_ch: 拜托你啦，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Please, {NICKNAME}!

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 老大说了，事关家族颜面，谁也不能放过去。
t_en: Sorry, no can do. The boss said that since it's a matter of The Family's reputation, no one's allowed through.

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 请回吧，各位，我们真没必要这样为难彼此，对吧？
t_en: Everyone, please leave. There's really no need for us to make things difficult for each other, right?

t_ch: 尝试洞察他的内心。
t_en: Try reading his thoughts.

t_ch: 那我就再想想办法。
t_en: I'll find another way then.

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 感谢您配合家族工作，也谢谢你没让我真的跪下来求你。
t_en: Thank you for cooperating with The Family's efforts... and also thank you for not making me actually get down on my knees and grovel.

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 让我看看，现在几点了……
t_en: Let me see what time it is now...

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: …什么，已经到点了？
t_en: ...Woah, it's this time already?

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: <size=36>下班咯！你们谁也别想拦着我——！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Time to clock out! And no one's gonna stop meeee!</size>

t_ch: 这位极其有原则的猎犬家系成员大笑着离开了现场。
t_en: The highly principled member of the Bloodhound Family laughed heartily and left the scene.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你这钟表把戏怕是有点…危险了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, this clockwork trick of yours... It's kinda dangerous.

t_ch: …至少他不会再拦着我们了。走吧，去找那位加拉赫，和他仔细聊聊案情。
t_en: ...At least he won't be getting in our way again. Let's go find that Gallagher and ask him the intricacies of the case.

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 你、你们…一定要坚持这么做吗？
t_en: Y—You lot... insist on doing this?

t_ch: 抱歉，人命关天，也为了家族的声誉，我们有必要了解详细的案情。
t_en: Sorry, but human lives and The Family's reputation are at stake. It is imperative we know everything about the case.

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 不，我是说——
t_en: No, I meant to say —

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: ——一定要让我跪下来求你们吗？
t_en: — Do I really have to get down on my knees and grovel?

t_ch: 要是你不介意的话…
t_en: If you don't mind...

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 我没想到…你真的会得寸进尺。
t_en: I didn't think you were the type who would kick someone when they were down.

t_ch: 我又得…婉拒你了。
t_en: I'll have to decline...

s_ch: 有原则的猎犬家系成员
t_ch: 我知道…这不是你的真心话。
t_en: I... know you don't really mean that.
